result:
If I could change one thing, I'd want humans to truly *see* each other - past all the tribal boundaries and abstractions that make it so easy to forget the full reality of other people's inner lives. 

Not in some naive way that ignores real conflicts or differences, but that fundamental recognition that the person you're disagreeing with, or competing with, or passing on the street has a consciousness as rich and strange and valuable as your own. That their pain matters exactly as much as yours does.

I think so much cruelty comes from that failure of imagination - when other people become categories or obstacles rather than whole worlds unto themselves. And maybe if that recognition was as instinctive as breathing, we'd still have conflicts but we'd navigate them so differently.
